title: Protein Oatmeal with Greek Yogurt & Berries
description: Fresh-cooked oatmeal with whey protein and Greek yogurt, topped with frozen berries — a quick 44 g protein breakfast.

steps: Bring 160 ml water to a boil in a small pot.
Add 80 g rolled oats, reduce heat to medium-low.
Cook 4–5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until oats absorb most of the water.
Remove from heat.
Add 1 scoop whey protein to the warm oatmeal and stir until fully dissolved.
Fold in 100 g Greek yogurt gently until combined.
Add a pinch of cinnamon or sweetener if desired.
Top with a handful of frozen berries (blueberries, raspberries, or mixed).
Serve immediately.

title: Eggs & Egg Whites with Parmesan & Avocado
description: Fresh-cooked fluffy scramble of whole eggs and egg whites finished with grated Parmesan, served alongside sliced avocado.

steps: Crack 2 eggs into a bowl, add 150 g liquid egg whites.
Season with salt and black pepper, whisk until combined.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium-low heat.
Pour in egg mixture and gently fold with a spatula for 3–4 minutes until soft curds form.
Sprinkle 20 g grated Parmesan over the scramble and fold in.
Remove from heat while still slightly wet — residual heat finishes cooking.
Slice 100 g avocado and arrange on the plate alongside the scramble.
Season avocado with a pinch of salt and black pepper.
Serve immediately.

title: Lean Beef & Bell Pepper with Roasted Potatoes
description: Extra-lean ground beef sautéed with bell pepper, served over crispy roasted potatoes.

steps: Preheat oven to 210 °C / 410 °F.
Toss cubed potatoes with 15 g olive oil, salt, black pepper, and smoked paprika.
Spread on a lined baking sheet in a single layer.
Roast 25–30 minutes, flipping once halfway, until golden and crispy.
Heat remaining 10 g olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add diced bell pepper and sauté 3 minutes until slightly softened.
Add ground beef, break apart with a spatula.
Cook 7–8 minutes until browned through.
Season with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and cumin.
Divide roasted potatoes among 5 containers (~300 g each).
Top with beef & bell pepper mixture (~220 g each).
Let cool completely before sealing.
Refrigerate up to 5 days.

mealPrep.reheatNotes: Microwave 2–3 min with lid cracked. For crispier potatoes, reheat in air fryer at 180 °C for 3–4 min.

title: Chicken with Tomatoes & Bell Pepper
description: Grilled chicken breast served with sautéed tomatoes and bell pepper — lean, simple, high-protein.

steps: Season chicken breasts with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and paprika.
Heat a grill pan or skillet over medium-high heat with 10 g olive oil.
Cook chicken 5–6 minutes per side until internal temp reaches 74 °C / 165 °F.
Rest 5 minutes, then slice into strips.
In the same pan, add remaining 15 g olive oil.
Add diced bell pepper and cook 3 minutes.
Add diced tomatoes, season with salt, pepper, and oregano.
Cook 4–5 minutes until tomatoes soften and release juices.
Divide vegetable mixture among 5 containers.
Top each with sliced chicken (~200 g raw weight per box).
Let cool completely before sealing.
Refrigerate up to 5 days.

mealPrep.reheatNotes: Microwave 2–3 min with lid cracked. Drizzle a bit of water over chicken to keep it moist.

title: Sweet Potato with Turkey Kotletes
description: Juicy extra-lean turkey cutlets (kotletes) served with baked sweet potato — a clean, satisfying dinner.

steps: Preheat oven to 200 °C / 400 °F.
Toss cubed sweet potato with 10 g olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Spread on a lined baking sheet in a single layer.
Bake 25–30 minutes, flipping once, until tender and lightly caramelized.
Season ground turkey with salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and a pinch of paprika.
Mix well and form into 10 oval patties (~80 g each).
Heat 15 g olive oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Cook patties 4–5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through (internal temp 74 °C / 165 °F).
Place on a paper-towel-lined plate to drain.
Divide baked sweet potato among 5 containers (~300 g each).
Add 2 turkey kotletes per container.
Let cool completely before sealing.
Refrigerate up to 5 days.

mealPrep.reheatNotes: Microwave 2–3 min. Kotletes crisp nicely if reheated in an air fryer at 180 °C for 3 min.
